sentences.sentence: The banana is among the world's oldest crops.
sentences.translation: 香蕉是世界上最古老的农作物之一。
difficult_points: among 在这里表示“属于……之列”，不是简单的空间位置。
oldest 使用最高级，说明香蕉位列最古老的作物之中。

expressions.meaning_zh: 位列世界上最古老的……之中

sentences.sentence: Agricultural scientists believe that the first edible banana was discovered around ten thousand years ago.
sentences.translation: 农业科学家认为，第一种可食用的香蕉大约在一万年前被发现。

structure.text: that the first edible banana was discovered around ten thousand years ago
was discovered around ten thousand years ago

difficult_points: that 引导宾语从句，作 believe 的宾语。
从句使用被动语态 was discovered，强调香蕉被人发现。

sentences.sentence: It has been at an evolutionary standstill ever since it was first propagated in the jungles of South-East Asia at the end of the last ice age.
sentences.translation: 自从上一个冰期末期它首次在东南亚丛林中被繁殖以来，其进化便一直停滞不前。

structure.text: It has been at an evolutionary standstill
ever since it was first propagated in the jungles of South-East Asia at the end of the last ice age
at the end of the last ice age

difficult_points: ever since 引导时间状语从句，并与主句现在完成时呼应。
it 指前文的可食用香蕉；was propagated 表示“被繁殖、栽培”。

sentences.sentence: Normally the wild banana, a giant jungle herb called Musa acuminata, contains a mass of hard seeds that make the fruit virtually inedible.
sentences.translation: 通常，野生香蕉是一种名为小果野蕉的巨型丛林草本植物，内部含有大量坚硬种子，几乎让果肉无法食用。

structure.text: a giant jungle herb called Musa acuminata
contains a mass of hard seeds
that make the fruit virtually inedible

difficult_points: 两个逗号之间是同位语，解释 wild banana 是什么植物。
that 引导定语从句修饰 hard seeds；make 后接“宾语＋补语”结构。

sentences.sentence: But now and then, hunter- gatherers must have discovered rare mutant plants that produced seedless, edible fruits.
sentences.translation: 不过，狩猎采集者偶尔一定曾发现过一些罕见的突变植株，它们能结出无籽且可食用的果实。

structure.text: must have discovered rare mutant plants

difficult_points: must have discovered 表示对过去情况的高度肯定推测。
that 引导定语从句，修饰 rare mutant plants。

sentences.sentence: Geneticists now know that the vast majority of these soft-fruited I plants resulted from genetic accidents that gave their cells three copies of each chromosome instead of the usual two.
sentences.translation: 遗传学家现在知道，这些果肉柔软的植株绝大多数源于遗传意外；这种意外使其细胞拥有每条染色体的三个副本，而不是通常的两个。

structure.text: that the vast majority of these soft-fruited I plants resulted from genetic accidents
the vast majority of these soft-fruited I plants
that gave their cells three copies of each chromosome instead of the usual two

difficult_points: 第一个 that 引导 know 的宾语从句，第二个 that 引导定语从句修饰 genetic accidents。
gave their cells three copies 是双宾语结构；原文中的 I 属于源文异常，需按 plants 理解。

sentences.sentence: This imbalance prevents seeds and pollen from developing normally, rendering the mutant plants sterile.
sentences.translation: 这种失衡会阻止种子和花粉正常发育，从而使突变植株无法繁殖。

structure.text: prevents seeds and pollen from developing normally

difficult_points: prevent A from doing 表示“阻止 A 做某事”。
rendering 是结果性现在分词短语，说明前述失衡造成的结果。

sentences.sentence: And that is why some scientists believe the world's most popular fruit could be doomed.
sentences.translation: 这正是一些科学家认为世界上最受欢迎的水果可能注定消亡的原因。

structure.text: why some scientists believe the world's most popular fruit could be doomed
the world's most popular fruit could be doomed

difficult_points: that 回指上一句所说的遗传失衡与不育问题。
why 引导表语从句；believe 后省略了宾语从句连接词 that。

sentences.sentence: It lacks the genetic diversity to fight off pests and diseases that are invading the banana plantations of Central America and the smallholdings of Africa and Asia alike.
sentences.translation: 它缺乏抵御病虫害所需的遗传多样性，而这些病虫害正侵袭着中美洲的香蕉种植园以及非洲和亚洲的小农场。

structure.text: to fight off pests and diseases
that are invading the banana plantations of Central America and the smallholdings of Africa and Asia alike

difficult_points: to fight off... 修饰 genetic diversity，说明这种多样性的用途。
that 引导定语从句修饰 pests and diseases；alike 表示两类地区同样受侵袭。

sentences.sentence: In some ways, the banana today resembles the potato before blight brought famine to Ireland a century and a half ago.
sentences.translation: 从某些方面看，今天的香蕉很像一个半世纪前疫病给爱尔兰带来饥荒之前的马铃薯。

structure.text: before blight brought famine to Ireland a century and a half ago

difficult_points: before 引导时间状语从句，以爱尔兰大饥荒前的马铃薯作类比。
brought famine to Ireland 是 bring A to B 结构，表示“给爱尔兰带来饥荒”。

expressions.text: a century and a half ago

sentences.sentence: But "it holds a lesson for other crops, too," says Emile Frison, top banana at the International Network for the Improvement of Banana and Plantain in
sentences.translation: 不过，埃米尔·弗里松说：“它也给其他作物提供了教训。”他是国际香蕉和大蕉改良网络的负责人；原文在机构所在地之前被段落截断。

structure.text: "it holds a lesson for other crops, too,"
holds a lesson for other crops
top banana at the International Network for the Improvement of Banana and Plantain in

difficult_points: it 指香蕉所面临的危机；hold a lesson 表示“蕴含可供借鉴的教训”。
top banana 是双关式表达，意为“负责人”；句尾 in 因源文跨段截断而悬空。

sentences.sentence: Montpellier, France.
sentences.translation: 法国蒙彼利埃。
difficult_points: 这是上一段被截断的机构所在地，完整意思应接在 B 段末尾的 in 之后。
原文把地点单独列为 C 段，因此这里必须保留为句子片段。

sentences.sentence: "The state of the banana," Frison warns, "can teach a broader lesson: the increasing standardisation of food crops round the world is threatening their ability to adapt and survive."
sentences.translation: 弗里松警告说：“香蕉的现状能带来一个更广泛的教训：世界各地粮食作物日益标准化，正在威胁它们适应环境和生存的能力。”

structure.text: "can teach a broader lesson: the increasing standardisation of food crops round the world is threatening their ability to adapt and survive."
the increasing standardisation of food crops round the world
is threatening their ability to adapt and survive

difficult_points: 引语被 Frison warns 插入分隔，前后仍组成一个完整陈述。
冒号后解释 broader lesson 的具体内容；their 指 food crops。

expressions.text: the ability to adapt and survive

sentences.sentence: The first Stone Age plant breeders cultivated these sterile freaks by replanting cuttings from their stems.
sentences.translation: 最早的石器时代植物育种者通过重新栽种这些不育植株的茎插条来培育它们。

structure.text: The first Stone Age plant breeders
by replanting cuttings from their stems

difficult_points: by doing 表示实现培育的方式。
these sterile freaks 指前文因染色体异常而不育的香蕉突变植株。

sentences.sentence: And the descendants of those original cuttings are the bananas we still eat today.
sentences.translation: 那些最初插条的后代，就是我们今天仍在食用的香蕉。

structure.text: the descendants of those original cuttings
the bananas we still eat today

difficult_points: we still eat today 是省略关系代词 that 的定语从句，修饰 bananas。
主语 descendants 强调今天的香蕉源于早期插条的无性繁殖。

sentences.sentence: Each is a virtual clone, almost devoid of genetic diversity.
sentences.translation: 每一株几乎都是克隆体，几乎没有遗传多样性。
difficult_points: Each 指每一株食用香蕉，谓语用单数 is。
devoid of 表示“完全缺乏”，almost 对其程度作限制。

sentences.sentence: And that uniformity makes it ripe for disease like no other crop on Earth.
sentences.translation: 这种高度一致性使香蕉比地球上任何其他作物都更容易受到疾病侵袭。

structure.text: like no other crop on Earth

difficult_points: it 指香蕉；make it ripe 是“动词＋宾语＋宾补”结构。
ripe for disease 在此不是“成熟”，而是“极易遭受疾病”。

sentences.sentence: Traditional varieties of sexually reproducing crops have always had a much broader genetic base, and the genes will recombine in new arrangements in each generation.
sentences.translation: 有性繁殖作物的传统品种一直拥有广泛得多的遗传基础，而且其基因会在每一代中重新组合成新的排列。

structure.text: Traditional varieties of sexually reproducing crops have always had a much broader genetic base
Traditional varieties of sexually reproducing crops
the genes will recombine in new arrangements in each generation

difficult_points: and 连接两个并列分句，分别说明遗传基础与代际重组。
sexually reproducing 是现在分词短语作定语，修饰 crops。

sentences.sentence: This gives them much greater flexibility in evolving responses to disease -and far more genetic resources to draw on in the face of an attack.
sentences.translation: 这使它们在进化出疾病应对机制方面灵活得多，也让它们在遭受侵袭时拥有丰富得多的遗传资源可供利用。

structure.text: to draw on in the face of an attack

difficult_points: This 回指上一句的广泛遗传基础与基因重组。
to draw on 修饰 genetic resources；原文 -and 连字符与空格异常需保留。

sentences.sentence: But that advantage is fading fast, as growers increasingly plant the same few, high-yielding varieties.
sentences.translation: 然而，随着种植者越来越多地种植少数几个相同的高产品种，这一优势正在迅速消失。

structure.text: as growers increasingly plant the same few, high-yielding varieties

difficult_points: that advantage 指传统品种拥有的遗传多样性优势。
as 引导伴随变化或原因状语从句，说明优势消失的背景。

sentences.sentence: Plant breeders work feverishly to maintain resistance in these standardised crops.
sentences.translation: 植物育种者正拼命努力，以维持这些标准化作物的抗病能力。

structure.text: to maintain resistance in these standardised crops

difficult_points: to maintain... 是目的不定式，说明育种者努力工作的目标。
resistance 在语境中指对病虫害的抵抗力。

sentences.sentence: Should these efforts falter, yields of even the most productive crop could swiftly crash.
sentences.translation: 如果这些努力失败，即使是产量最高的作物，其收成也可能迅速暴跌。

structure.text: yields of even the most productive crop could swiftly crash

difficult_points: Should these efforts falter 是省略 if 的虚拟条件倒装，相当于 If these efforts should falter。
crash 在这里指产量急剧下降，而非交通事故。

sentences.sentence: "When some pest or disease comes along, severe epidemics can occur," says Geoff Hawtin, director of the Rome-based
sentences.translation: 杰夫·霍廷说：“一旦某种害虫或疾病出现，就可能爆发严重疫情。”他是这家总部位于罗马的机构负责人；机构名称被截到下一段。

structure.text: "When some pest or disease comes along, severe epidemics can occur,"
When some pest or disease comes along

difficult_points: when 引导时间条件，表示病虫害一旦出现所引发的后果。
Rome-based 后缺少中心名词，是源文跨段截断造成的。

sentences.sentence: International Plant Genetic Resources Institute.
sentences.translation: 国际植物遗传资源研究所。
difficult_points: 该机构名称承接 D 段末尾的 the Rome-based。
原文将名称单独划为 E 段，因此需保留这一不完整句。

sentences.sentence: The banana is an excellent case in point.
sentences.translation: 香蕉就是一个绝佳的例证。
difficult_points: case in point 是固定表达，表示能直接证明前述观点的例子。
本句承上启下，将作物标准化的风险具体落实到香蕉。

sentences.sentence: Until the 1950s, one variety, the Gros Michel, dominated the world's commercial banana business.
sentences.translation: 直到20世纪50年代，大麦克香蕉这一品种一直主导着全球商业香蕉市场。

structure.text: dominated the world's commercial banana business

difficult_points: the Gros Michel 是 one variety 的同位语，给出品种名称。
dominated 表示该品种在市场中占据主导地位。

sentences.sentence: Found by French botanists in Asia in the 1820s, the Gros Michel was by all accounts a fine banana, richer and sweeter than today's standard banana and without the latter's bitter aftertaste when green.
sentences.translation: 大麦克香蕉于19世纪20年代由法国植物学家在亚洲发现；据所有说法，它都是一种上佳香蕉，比今天的标准香蕉更浓郁、更甜，而且未成熟时也没有后者的苦涩余味。

structure.text: Found by French botanists in Asia in the 1820s
was by all accounts a fine banana
richer and sweeter than today's standard banana
without the latter's bitter aftertaste when green

difficult_points: Found... 是过去分词短语，其逻辑主语是 the Gros Michel。
the latter 指 today's standard banana；when green 表示香蕉未成熟时。

sentences.sentence: But it was vulnerable to a soil fungus that produced a wilt known as Panama disease.
sentences.translation: 但它很容易受到一种土壤真菌侵袭，这种真菌会引发被称为巴拿马病的枯萎病。

structure.text: was vulnerable to a soil fungus
that produced a wilt known as Panama disease

difficult_points: it 指大麦克香蕉；be vulnerable to 表示容易受某物伤害。
that 修饰 soil fungus；known as... 又修饰 a wilt。

sentences.sentence: "Once the fungus gets into the soil, it remains there for many years.
sentences.translation: “真菌一旦进入土壤，就会在那里存留多年。

structure.text: Once the fungus gets into the soil
it remains there for many years

difficult_points: once 引导时间兼条件状语从句，表示“一旦……”。
it 指 the fungus；引号在本句开头、到后文 F-07 才闭合。

sentences.sentence: There is nothing farmers can do.
sentences.translation: 农民对此无能为力。
difficult_points: There is nothing... 表示“不存在任何……”。
farmers can do 是省略关系代词 that 的定语从句，修饰 nothing。

sentences.sentence: Even chemical spraying won't get rid of it," says Rodomiro Ortiz, director of the International Institute for Tropical Agriculture in Ibadan, Nigeria.
sentences.translation: “即使喷洒化学药剂也无法清除它。”尼日利亚伊巴丹国际热带农业研究所所长罗多米罗·奥尔蒂斯说。

structure.text: Even chemical spraying won't get rid of it
director of the International Institute for Tropical Agriculture in Ibadan, Nigeria

difficult_points: it 指进入土壤的真菌；even 强调连化学喷洒也无效。
director... 是 Rodomiro Ortiz 的同位语，说明其身份。

sentences.sentence: So plantation owners played a running game, abandoning infested fields and moving to "clean" land - until they ran out of clean land in the 1950s and had to abandon the Gros Michel.
sentences.translation: 于是种植园主打起了游击战：放弃受感染的田地，转移到“干净”的土地；直到20世纪50年代再也找不到干净土地，只得放弃大麦克香蕉。

structure.text: plantation owners played a running game
abandoning infested fields and moving to "clean" land
until they ran out of clean land in the 1950s and had to abandon the Gros Michel
had to abandon the Gros Michel

difficult_points: 两个现在分词说明 played a running game 的具体做法。
until 引导时间状语从句，其中 ran out of 与 had to abandon 并列。

sentences.sentence: Its successor, and still the reigning commercial king, is the Cavendish banana, a 19th-century British discovery from southern China.
sentences.translation: 它的继任者、至今仍占商业统治地位的品种，是卡文迪什香蕉——一种19世纪由英国人从中国南方发现的香蕉。

structure.text: and still the reigning commercial king
a 19th-century British discovery from southern China

difficult_points: Its 指大麦克香蕉；successor 与 commercial king 都描述卡文迪什。
句末同位语说明卡文迪什被英国人发现的时代与来源地。

expressions.meaning_zh: 当前占商业主导地位的品种

sentences.sentence: The Cavendish is resistant to Panama disease and, as a result, it literally saved the international banana industry.
sentences.translation: 卡文迪什香蕉能够抵抗巴拿马病，因此可以说它拯救了国际香蕉产业。

structure.text: The Cavendish is resistant to Panama disease
it literally saved the international banana industry

difficult_points: and 连接因果相关的两个分句，as a result 明示结果。
it 指 the Cavendish；literally 强调这里并非夸张。

sentences.sentence: During the 1960s, it replaced the Gros Michel on supermarket shelves.
sentences.translation: 20世纪60年代，卡文迪什香蕉取代大麦克香蕉登上了超市货架。
difficult_points: it 指卡文迪什香蕉。
replace A 表示“取代 A”，不需要再加介词 with。

sentences.sentence: If you buy a banana today, it is almost certainly a Cavendish.
sentences.translation: 如果你今天买一根香蕉，它几乎肯定是卡文迪什品种。

structure.text: If you buy a banana today
it is almost certainly a Cavendish

difficult_points: if 引导真实条件句，用一般现在时描述普遍情况。
it 指所购买的那根香蕉；almost certainly 表示极高概率。

sentences.sentence: But even so, it is a minority in the world's banana crop.
sentences.translation: 但即便如此，卡文迪什在全球香蕉作物中仍只占少数。
difficult_points: even so 表示让步转折：商业市场占主导并不等于全球产量占多数。
it 仍指卡文迪什品种。

sentences.sentence: Half a billion people in Asia and Africa depend on bananas.
sentences.translation: 亚洲和非洲有五亿人依赖香蕉为生。

structure.text: Half a billion people in Asia and Africa

difficult_points: Half a billion 即五亿，而不是五千万。
depend on 在此不仅是喜欢，而是生活与食物来源上的依赖。

sentences.sentence: Bananas provide the largest source of calories and are eaten daily.
sentences.translation: 香蕉是他们最大的热量来源，也是每日食用的食物。

structure.text: provide the largest source of calories

difficult_points: provide 与 are eaten 是由 and 连接的并列谓语。
are eaten 使用被动语态，省略了食用者。

sentences.sentence: Its name is synonymous with food.
sentences.translation: 在那里，香蕉这个名称几乎就等同于食物。
difficult_points: Its 指香蕉的；并非指前一句的 calories。
synonymous with 在这里是强调香蕉与基本食物密不可分。

sentences.sentence: But the day of reckoning may be coming for the Cavendish and its indigenous kin.
sentences.translation: 但卡文迪什及其本土近缘品种可能很快就要迎来清算之日。

structure.text: for the Cavendish and its indigenous kin

difficult_points: day of reckoning 是比喻，指必须面对严重后果的时刻。
its indigenous kin 指卡文迪什在各地的本土近缘香蕉品种。

expressions.meaning_zh: 清算或面临后果的时刻

sentences.sentence: Another fungal disease, black Sigatoka, has become a global epidemic since its first appearance in Fiji in 1963.
sentences.translation: 另一种真菌病——黑叶斑病——自1963年首次在斐济出现以来，已经演变成全球性流行病。

structure.text: since its first appearance in Fiji in 1963

difficult_points: black Sigatoka 是 Another fungal disease 的同位语。
since 与现在完成时 has become 搭配，标明变化的起点。

sentences.sentence: Left to itself, black Sigatoka- which causes brown wounds on leaves and premature fruit ripening - cuts fruit yields by 50 to 70 per cent and reduces the productive lifetime of banana plants from 30 years to as little as 2 or 3.
sentences.translation: 如果任其发展，黑叶斑病——它会造成叶片褐色伤斑和果实过早成熟——会使果实产量下降50%至70%，并把香蕉植株的生产寿命从30年缩短到仅仅两三年。

structure.text: which causes brown wounds on leaves and premature fruit ripening
cuts fruit yields by 50 to 70 per cent
reduces the productive lifetime of banana plants from 30 years to as little as 2 or 3

difficult_points: Left to itself 是条件性的过去分词短语，意为“如果任其发展”。
which 是插入的非限制性定语从句；cuts 与 reduces 构成并列谓语。

sentences.sentence: Commercial growers keep black Sigatoka at bay by a massive chemical assault.
sentences.translation: 商业种植者通过大规模使用化学药剂来遏制黑叶斑病。
difficult_points: keep ... at bay 是固定搭配，表示阻止威胁逼近或恶化。
by 介词短语说明控制病害所采用的手段。

sentences.sentence: Forty sprayings of fungicide a year is typical.
sentences.translation: 一年喷洒四十次杀菌剂是很常见的做法。

structure.text: Forty sprayings of fungicide a year

difficult_points: 整个数量短语被视为一种典型做法，因此原文谓语用单数 is。
a year 表示频率，即“每年”。

sentences.sentence: But despite the fungicides, diseases such as black Sigatoka are getting more and more difficult to control.
sentences.translation: 然而，尽管使用了杀菌剂，黑叶斑病等疾病仍变得越来越难以控制。

structure.text: are getting more and more difficult to control

difficult_points: despite 后接名词短语，不是从句，表达让步。
to control 的逻辑宾语是 diseases，属于“形容词＋不定式”结构。

expressions.text: more and more difficult to control

sentences.sentence: "As soon as you bring in a new fun gicide, they develop resistance," says Frison.
sentences.translation: 弗里松说：“你一引进新的杀菌剂，它们就会产生抗药性。”

structure.text: As soon as you bring in a new fun gicide

difficult_points: as soon as 表示“一……就……”，强调抗药性出现得很快。
they 指病原体或疾病；fun gicide 是原文被错误拆开的 fungicide。

sentences.sentence: "One thing we can be sure of is that black Sigatoka won't lose in this battle." Poor farmers, who cannot afford chemicals, have it even worse.
sentences.translation: “有一点我们可以肯定：黑叶斑病不会在这场较量中落败。”而买不起化学药剂的贫困农民处境更加糟糕。

structure.text: "One thing we can be sure of is that black Sigatoka won't lose in this battle."
that black Sigatoka won't lose in this battle
Poor farmers, who cannot afford chemicals, have it even worse

difficult_points: 本条按原文保留为引语句与后续陈述句的组合；两者语义相承。
we can be sure of 省略关系代词，修饰 One thing；who 修饰 Poor farmers。

sentences.sentence: They cap do little more than watch their plants die.
sentences.translation: 他们几乎只能眼睁睁看着自己的植株死去。

structure.text: cap do little more than watch their plants die

difficult_points: They 指买不起化学药剂的贫困农民。
cap 是源文 OCR 异常，按上下文应理解为 can；watch 后是“宾语＋不带 to 的不定式”。

expressions.meaning_zh: 除了……几乎什么也做不了

sentences.sentence: "Most of the banana fields in Amazonia have already been destroyed by the disease," says Luadir Gasparotto, Brazil's leading banana pathologist with the government research agency EMBRAPA.
sentences.translation: 巴西政府研究机构 EMBRAPA 的首席香蕉病理学家卢阿迪尔·加斯帕罗托说：“亚马孙地区的大多数香蕉田已经被这种疾病摧毁。”

structure.text: "Most of the banana fields in Amazonia have already been destroyed by the disease,"
Most of the banana fields in Amazonia
have already been destroyed by the disease
Brazil's leading banana pathologist with the government research agency EMBRAPA

difficult_points: 引语使用现在完成时被动语态，强调破坏已经发生并影响现在。
句末长同位语说明 Luadir Gasparotto 的专业身份与任职机构。

sentences.sentence: Production is likely to fall by 70 per cent as the disease spreads, he predicts.
sentences.translation: 他预测，随着疾病蔓延，产量很可能下降70%。

structure.text: Production is likely to fall by 70 per cent as the disease spreads
is likely to fall by 70 per cent

difficult_points: he predicts 是后置报告语，he 指上一句的 Gasparotto。
as 引导伴随变化状语从句；by 70 per cent 表示下降幅度。

sentences.sentence: The only option will be to find a new variety.
sentences.translation: 唯一的选择将是找到一个新品种。
difficult_points: to find... 是不定式作表语，具体说明 option 的内容。
will 表明作者对未来解决路径的判断。

sentences.sentence: But how?
sentences.translation: 但要怎么做呢？
difficult_points: 这是省略句，完整含义是“但要怎样找到一个新品种？”
But 承接上一段的唯一选择，并引出实际困难。

sentences.sentence: Almost all edible varieties are susceptible to the diseases, so growers cannot simply change to a different banana.
sentences.translation: 几乎所有可食用品种都容易感染这些疾病，所以种植者不能简单地换种另一种香蕉。

structure.text: Almost all edible varieties are susceptible to the diseases
so growers cannot simply change to a different banana

difficult_points: so 连接结果分句，前因是几乎所有可食用品种都易感病。
be susceptible to 表示“易受……影响或感染”。

sentences.sentence: With most crops, such a threat would unleash an army of breeders, scouring the world for resistant relatives whose traits they can breed into commercial varieties.
sentences.translation: 对于大多数作物，这类威胁会促使大批育种者出动，在全球搜寻具有抗性的近缘品种，再把它们的性状育入商业品种。

structure.text: would unleash an army of breeders
scouring the world for resistant relatives
whose traits they can breed into commercial varieties

difficult_points: scouring... 的逻辑主语是 breeders，说明他们被调动后所做的事。
whose 引导定语从句修饰 resistant relatives，whose traits 作 breed 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: Not so with the banana.
sentences.translation: 但香蕉却不是这样。
difficult_points: 这是省略句，否定上一句所述的常规育种路径适用于香蕉。
so 代指“能动员育种者寻找抗病近缘种并杂交”的情况。

sentences.sentence: Because all edible varieties are sterile, bringing in new genetic traits to help cope with pests and diseases is nearly impossible.
sentences.translation: 由于所有可食用品种都不育，要引入新的遗传性状来帮助应对病虫害几乎不可能。

structure.text: Because all edible varieties are sterile
bringing in new genetic traits to help cope with pests and diseases
to help cope with pests and diseases

difficult_points: Because 引导原因状语从句，解释常规育种为何难以进行。
bringing in... 是动名词短语作主语；to help... 说明引入性状的目的。

sentences.sentence: Nearly, but not totally.
sentences.translation: 是几乎不可能，但并非完全不可能。
difficult_points: 本句省略了 impossible，承接上一句的 nearly impossible。
but not totally 修正前面的判断，为极少见的育种机会埋下伏笔。

sentences.sentence: Very rarely, a sterile banana will experience a genetic accident that allows an almost normal seed to develop, giving breeders a tiny window for improvement.
sentences.translation: 极少数情况下，不育香蕉会发生一次遗传意外，使一颗近乎正常的种子得以发育，从而给育种者留下一个极小的改良机会。

structure.text: that allows an almost normal seed to develop
giving breeders a tiny window for improvement

difficult_points: that 修饰 genetic accident；allow 后是“宾语＋to do”结构。
giving... 是结果性分词短语，说明这种意外带来的育种机会。

sentences.sentence: Breeders at the Honduran Foundation of Agricultural Research have tried to exploit this to create disease-resistant varieties.
sentences.translation: 洪都拉斯农业研究基金会的育种者一直尝试利用这一机会培育抗病品种。

structure.text: Breeders at the Honduran Foundation of Agricultural Research

difficult_points: this 指上一句所说的正常种子偶尔能够发育的机会。
to create... 是目的不定式，说明利用该机会的目标。

sentences.sentence: Further back-crossing with wild bananas yielded a new seedless banana resistant to both black Sigatoka and Panama disease.
sentences.translation: 与野生香蕉进一步回交，培育出了一种同时抵抗黑叶斑病和巴拿马病的新品种无籽香蕉。

structure.text: resistant to both black Sigatoka and Panama disease

difficult_points: back-crossing 是名词化的育种过程，在句中作主语。
resistant to... 是后置形容词短语，修饰 a new seedless banana。

sentences.sentence: Neither Western supermarket consumers nor peas ant growers like the new hybrid.
sentences.translation: 西方超市消费者和农民种植者都不喜欢这种新杂交品种。

structure.text: Neither Western supermarket consumers nor peas ant growers

difficult_points: neither...nor... 表示两类人都不喜欢该品种。
peas ant 是源文 OCR 断裂，结合语境应按 peasant growers 理解。

sentences.sentence: Some accuse it of tasting more like an apple than a banana.
sentences.translation: 有人指责它吃起来更像苹果而不像香蕉。

structure.text: accuse it of tasting more like an apple than a banana
of tasting more like an apple than a banana

difficult_points: it 指新杂交香蕉；accuse A of doing 表示“指责 A 做某事或具有某特点”。
more like A than B 表示“与其说像 B，不如说像 A”。

sentences.sentence: Not surprisingly, the majority of plant breeders have till now turned their backs on the banana and got to work on easier plants.
sentences.translation: 不出所料，到目前为止，大多数植物育种者都已放弃香蕉，转而研究更容易处理的植物。

structure.text: have till now turned their backs on the banana
got to work on easier plants

difficult_points: turned 与 got 是由 and 连接、共享主语和 have 的并列动作。
turn one's back on 在此表示放弃、不再支持，而非字面转身。

sentences.sentence: And commercial banana companies are now washing their hands of the whole breeding effort, preferring to fund a search for new fungicides instead.
sentences.translation: 商业香蕉公司如今也对整个育种工作撒手不管，转而更愿意资助寻找新的杀菌剂。

structure.text: are now washing their hands of the whole breeding effort
preferring to fund a search for new fungicides instead
to fund a search for new fungicides

difficult_points: wash one's hands of 是习语，表示不再负责或参与。
preferring... 是伴随分词短语，说明公司转向的替代方案。

sentences.sentence: "We supported a breeding programme for 40 years, but it wasn't able to develop an alternative to the Cavendish.
sentences.translation: “我们支持一个育种项目长达40年，但它始终没能培育出可替代卡文迪什的品种。

structure.text: We supported a breeding programme for 40 years
but it wasn't able to develop an alternative to the Cavendish
to develop an alternative to the Cavendish

difficult_points: but 连接投入与结果之间的强烈转折。
it 指 a breeding programme；引语在下一句末尾才闭合。

sentences.sentence: It was very expensive and we got nothing back," says Ronald Romero, head of research at Chiquita, one of the Big Three companies that dominate the international banana trade.
sentences.translation: “项目成本非常高，我们却毫无回报。”奇基塔公司研究主管罗纳德·罗梅罗说；该公司是主导国际香蕉贸易的三大公司之一。

structure.text: It was very expensive and we got nothing back
one of the Big Three companies that dominate the international banana trade
that dominate the international banana trade

difficult_points: It 指前一句的育种项目；got nothing back 表示投资没有取得回报。
that 引导定语从句修饰 the Big Three companies，说明其市场地位。

sentences.sentence: Last year, a global consortium of scientists led by Frison announced plans to sequence the banana genome within five years.
sentences.translation: 去年，一个由弗里松领导的全球科学家联盟宣布，计划在五年内完成香蕉基因组测序。

structure.text: a global consortium of scientists led by Frison
to sequence the banana genome within five years

difficult_points: led by Frison 是过去分词短语，修饰 consortium of scientists。
to sequence... 修饰 plans，说明计划的具体内容。

sentences.sentence: It would be the first edible fruit to be sequenced.
sentences.translation: 香蕉将成为第一种完成基因组测序的可食用水果。

structure.text: would be the first edible fruit

difficult_points: It 指香蕉；would 表示按该计划实现后的预期结果。
to be sequenced 是被动不定式，后置修饰 the first edible fruit。

sentences.sentence: Well, almost edible.
sentences.translation: 不过，严格说来只是“几乎可食用”。
difficult_points: 这是自我修正式省略句，修正上一句 edible fruit 的说法。
almost edible 为下句揭示实际测序对象是不可食用野生香蕉作铺垫。

sentences.sentence: The group will actually be sequencing inedible wild bananas from East Asia because many of these are resistant to black Sigatoka.
sentences.translation: 这个团队实际要测序的是来自东亚、不可食用的野生香蕉，因为其中许多品种能够抵抗黑叶斑病。

structure.text: will actually be sequencing inedible wild bananas from East Asia
because many of these are resistant to black Sigatoka

difficult_points: future progressive will be sequencing 强调计划中实际将开展的工作。
these 指 inedible wild bananas；because 从句解释为何选择它们。

sentences.sentence: If they can pinpoint the genes that help these wild varieties to resist black Sigatoka, the protective genes could be introduced into laboratory tissue cultures of cells from edible varieties.
sentences.translation: 如果科学家能准确找出帮助这些野生品种抵抗黑叶斑病的基因，就可以把这些保护性基因导入可食用品种细胞的实验室组织培养物中。

structure.text: If they can pinpoint the genes that help these wild varieties to resist black Sigatoka
that help these wild varieties to resist black Sigatoka
could be introduced into laboratory tissue cultures of cells from edible varieties

difficult_points: that 引导定语从句修饰 genes；help 后接“宾语＋to do”。
主句使用 could be introduced 的被动结构，说明基因被导入培养组织。

sentences.sentence: These could then be propagated into new disease-resistant plants and passed on to farmers.
sentences.translation: 随后，这些培养物可以被繁殖成新的抗病植株，再提供给农民。

structure.text: could then be propagated into new disease-resistant plants

difficult_points: These 指上一句的 laboratory tissue cultures，而非 protective genes。
be propagated 与 passed on 共享 could be，构成两个并列被动谓语。

sentences.sentence: It sounds promising, but the big banana companies have, until now, refused to get involved in GM research for fear of alienating their customers.
sentences.translation: 这个方案听起来很有希望，但到目前为止，大型香蕉公司一直拒绝参与转基因研究，因为担心疏远消费者。

structure.text: the big banana companies have, until now, refused to get involved in GM research
for fear of alienating their customers

difficult_points: It 指上一段的基因测序和基因导入方案。
for fear of doing 表示“因为害怕……”，说明公司拒绝参与的原因。

sentences.sentence: "Biotechnology is extremely expensive and there are serious questions about consumer acceptance," says David McLaughlin, Chiquita's senior director for environ- mental affairs.
sentences.translation: 奇基塔公司负责环境事务的高级主管戴维·麦克劳克林说：“生物技术成本极高，而且消费者能否接受还存在严重疑问。”

structure.text: "Biotechnology is extremely expensive and there are serious questions about consumer acceptance,"
there are serious questions about consumer acceptance
Chiquita's senior director for environ- mental affairs

difficult_points: 引语内 and 连接技术成本与消费者接受度两个问题。
environ- mental 是源文断词，应结合语境理解为 environmental。

sentences.sentence: With scant funding from the companies, the banana genome researchers are focusing on the other end of the spectrum.
sentences.translation: 由于企业提供的资金寥寥无几，香蕉基因组研究人员正把重点放在光谱的另一端，也就是小农户的需求上。

structure.text: With scant funding from the companies
are focusing on the other end of the spectrum

difficult_points: With 短语说明研究所处的资金背景，也隐含原因。
the other end of the spectrum 与前文大公司相对，指后文的小农户一端。

expressions.text: the other end of the spectrum

sentences.sentence: Even if they can identify the crucial genes, they will be a long way from developing new varieties that smallholders will find suitable and affordable.
sentences.translation: 即使研究人员能识别出关键基因，他们距离培育出让小农户觉得适用且负担得起的新品种仍有很长的路。

structure.text: Even if they can identify the crucial genes
they will be a long way from developing new varieties
that smallholders will find suitable and affordable
smallholders will find suitable and affordable

difficult_points: Even if 引导让步条件从句，强调找到基因仍远非完成任务。
that 修饰 new varieties；find 后省略重复宾语 that，suitable and affordable 作宾补。

expressions.text: be a long way from doing

sentences.sentence: But whatever biotechnology's academic interest, it is the only hope for the banana.
sentences.translation: 但无论生物技术在学术上有何意义，它都是香蕉唯一的希望。

structure.text: is the only hope for the banana

difficult_points: whatever 引导让步结构，意为“不论其学术意义是什么”。
it 指 biotechnology，而不是 academic interest。

sentences.sentence: Without it, banana production worldwide will head into a tailspin.
sentences.translation: 没有生物技术，全球香蕉生产将陷入急剧失控的衰退。
difficult_points: it 指 biotechnology；Without it 相当于否定条件。
tailspin 原指飞机螺旋下坠，此处比喻产业迅速失控衰退。

sentences.sentence: We may even see the extinction of the banana as both a lifesaver for hungry and impoverished Africans and the most popular product on the world's supermarket shelves.
sentences.translation: 我们甚至可能看到香蕉走向消失——它将不再既是饥饿贫困的非洲人的救命食物，也不再是全球超市货架上最受欢迎的商品。

structure.text: as both a lifesaver for hungry and impoverished Africans and the most popular product on the world's supermarket shelves

difficult_points: see the extinction of... 表示目睹某物灭绝或消失。
as both A and B 同时说明香蕉可能丧失的两重角色：救命食物与热门商品。
